docs.title: Purdue Proton
docs.text: Purdue Proton is an educational RP2350B devboard with a detachable debug probe intended for use in embedded systems labs.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• RP2350B microcontroller
    • 520 kB SRAM
    • QFN-80 package
  • 16 MB quad SPI flash memory
  • Debugger based on Raspberry Pi Debug Probe design
  • USB-C connector on Proton board and debugger with ESD protection diodes
  • External 12 MHz crystal produces 150 MHz with onboard PLL
  • External 5V, 3.3V, and GND pins
  • Power indicator LEDs
    • 5V is red
    • 3V3 is green
  • 48 GPIO pins, external SWCLK/SWD pins
  • Push buttons for USB boot and reset
  • Two hardware-debounced push buttons connected to GP21 and GP26
  • Four user LEDs connected to GP22 (red), GP23 (yellow), GP24 (green), GP25 (blue)

docs.title: Buy a Proton
docs.text: Purchase links will be made available once boards have been finalized.

docs.title: Schematics, Pinouts, and Datasheets
docs.text: You can find the schematics, pinouts, and datasheets for the Purdue Proton here.

docs.title: Lab Experiments
docs.text: Purdue Proton is used by embedded systems labs in ECE 36200 at Purdue University. Their labs can be found here. The labs cover embedded programming with the Pico SDK in C utilizing the PlatformIO IDE.

docs.title: Powered by Raspberry Pi
docs.text:
Purdue Proton is part of the Powered by Pi scheme.

Our certificate can be found here.

docs.title: Schematics, Pinouts, and Datasheets
docs.text:
Hint

To open a page in a new tab, hold Ctrl/Cmd and click on the link!

docs.title: Raspberry Pi Resources
docs.text:
RP2350 Datasheet

  • Describes chip architecture, peripherals, and electrical characteristics.

Pico C/C++ SDK

  • Includes libraries and examples for using the RP2040 and RP2350 microcontrollers.

Hardware Design Guide for RP2350

  • Resource for designing a board with the RP2350 microcontroller.

docs.title: Raspberry Pi Debug Probe Resources
docs.text:
Debug Probe Schematics

  • Schematic for the Raspberry Pi Debug Probe, also used for the Proton debugger.

Raspberry Pi debugprobe firmware

  • The `debugprobe.uf2` file can be dropped directly into a Proton debug probe in USB boot mode.
